title: Malicious PyPI Package Targets macOS Developers with Hidden Cryptocurrency Miner
description: Datadog Security Labs identified a malicious PyPI package specifically targeting macOS developer machines, executing a hidden cryptocurrency mining process that ran silently in the background while consuming system resources.
label: AV-400

title: Docker Builder Cache Poisoning via Typosquatted Image Names in CI Builds
description: A Docker cache poisoning vulnerability allowed attackers using typosquatted image names to cause CI pipelines to execute untrusted cached layers, potentially injecting malicious code into build artifacts without developer awareness.
label: AV-200

title: Snyk Documents Dependency Confusion Attacks Targeting Scoped npm Packages
description: Snyk researchers documented dependency confusion attacks specifically targeting scoped npm packages where public packages with matching scoped names override private internal dependencies, exploiting npm registry resolution priority to execute attacker-controlled code.
label: AV-509

title: Checkmarx Documents Trojan Source Attack Altering Loop Behavior via Unicode
description: Checkmarx researchers documented the Trojan Source attack technique where hidden Unicode characters alter loop execution behavior, causing code that appears syntactically correct to execute fundamentally different logic than what developers see during review.
label: AV-300

title: Codecov Bash Uploader Compromised to Exfiltrate CI Environment Credentials
description: Attackers modified Codecov's bash uploader script used in CI pipelines to silently collect and exfiltrate environment variables including AWS credentials and tokens, affecting thousands of organizations that used the script in their build processes.
label: AV-410

title: PyTorch-nightly torchtriton Dependency Hijacked to Steal System Credentials
description: Attackers uploaded a malicious torchtriton package to PyPI matching an internal PyTorch dependency, hijacking nightly builds and causing affected systems to execute credential-stealing code with full system privileges through dependency confusion.
label: AV-400

title: ReversingLabs Identifies Malicious PyPI Packages Using Character Substitution Typosquatting
description: ReversingLabs researchers identified malicious PyPI packages using subtle character substitution techniques to create package names visually similar to legitimate libraries, deceiving developers into installing credential-stealing malware.
label: AV-200

title: JFrog Documents npm Dependency Confusion via Fallback Registry Exploitation
description: JFrog researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting npm fallback registry mechanisms where systems automatically resolve to public packages when private registry packages are unavailable, allowing attackers to intercept dependency resolution.
label: AV-509

title: Snyk Documents Trojan Source Unicode Trick Modifying Conditional Statement Logic
description: Snyk researchers documented the Trojan Source technique where hidden Unicode encoding changes conditional logic execution, causing if-else statements and boolean conditions to evaluate opposite to their visual appearance in code editors and review tools.
label: AV-300

title: GitHub Security Lab Documents pwn-requests Attack Pattern in GitHub Actions
description: GitHub Security Lab documented the pwn-requests attack pattern where GitHub Actions workflows using pull_request_target with checkout of untrusted code allow attackers to execute encoded malicious payloads with repository write permissions and secret access.
label: AV-410

title: dYdX Malicious npm Package Fetches Remote Cryptocurrency Wallet Stealer Payload
description: Threat actors published malicious dYdX client packages to npm that downloaded and executed remote cryptocurrency wallet-stealing payloads after installation, targeting quantitative finance developers using the dYdX decentralized exchange platform.
label: AV-400

title: Dependency Confusion Attack Compromises Apple Microsoft Netflix and Tesla Build Systems
description: Researcher Alex Birsan exploited dependency confusion by registering public packages at higher versions matching internal dependency names at 35 major organizations including Apple, Microsoft, Netflix, and Tesla, causing their build systems to automatically pull and execute attacker-controlled code.
label: AV-200

title: OWASP Documents Dependency Confusion as Top Supply Chain Security Risk
description: OWASP documented dependency confusion as a critical supply chain security vulnerability where internal package names leaked through public repositories allow attackers to upload matching public packages that override private dependency resolution in enterprise build systems.
label: AV-509

title: JFrog Documents Trojan Source Unicode Override Bypassing Security Checks
description: JFrog researchers documented Trojan Source attacks where Unicode override characters alter code to bypass security checks, causing authentication and authorization logic to execute opposite to its intended behavior while appearing completely valid to human reviewers.
label: AV-300

title: Nx Build System npm Package Compromised with Environment Variable Exfiltration Malware
description: The popular Nx monorepo build system npm package was compromised with malicious code that scanned for and exfiltrated environment variables and secrets from enterprise development teams using the tool across thousands of repositories.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious npm Package Scans System for Browser Session Tokens
description: A malicious npm package discovered in the GitHub Advisory Database contained code that scanned victim systems for browser session tokens and authentication cookies, silently extracting and transmitting them to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: Socket.dev Research Identifies Malicious Crypto Utility Packages Stealing Private Keys
description: Socket.dev security researchers identified malicious npm packages mimicking cryptocurrency utility tools that silently exfiltrated wallet private keys and seed phrases from developer machines, targeting Web3 and blockchain application developers.
label: AV-200

title: Unit42 Documents Dependency Confusion via Package Version Precedence Exploitation
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting package manager version precedence rules where higher-versioned public packages automatically supersede lower-versioned private internal dependencies in enterprise build environments.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Examples Repository Demonstrates Unicode Loop Injection Attacks
description: The trojan-source GitHub repository demonstrates real Trojan Source attack vectors including Unicode-concealed malicious loop injections where hidden control characters cause loop bodies to execute completely different code than what appears in the source file.
label: AV-300

title: Datadog Documents Lessons from Recent npm Package Compromise Supply Chain Attacks
description: Datadog Security Labs documented lessons from recent npm supply chain compromises where attackers injected malicious artifacts into CI/CD workflows, analyzing attack patterns and providing mitigation strategies for organizations using npm in their build pipelines.
label: AV-410

title: ReversingLabs Documents Malware Obfuscation in Malicious pip Packages
description: ReversingLabs researchers documented obfuscation techniques used in malicious PyPI packages including base64-encoded payloads and multi-stage loaders that initiate reverse shell connections after installation, evading static analysis detection.
label: AV-400

title: Phylum Discovers Malicious npm Package Campaign Using Express Framework Typosquatting
description: Phylum researchers discovered a malicious npm package campaign using typosquatting against the Express web framework, publishing packages with near-identical names that executed malicious code during installation targeting Node.js web developers.
label: AV-200

title: GitHub Advisory Database Documents Dependency Confusion via Registry Misconfiguration
description: Multiple GitHub Security Advisories document dependency confusion vulnerabilities arising from incorrect private registry configurations that allow public package registries to override internal dependencies, enabling attackers to inject malicious code into enterprise build systems.
label: AV-509

title: Kaspersky Documents Trojan Source Unicode Obfuscation Hiding Credential Theft Code
description: Kaspersky security researchers documented Trojan Source attack cases where hidden Unicode characters mask credential-stealing code, making malicious functions appear as legitimate utility code during code review while executing data exfiltration at runtime.
label: AV-300

title: Unit42 Documents Shai-Hulud npm Supply Chain Attack Injecting Unauthorized Build Steps
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented the Shai-Hulud npm supply chain attack where threat actors injected unauthorized build steps into CI/CD pipelines through compromised npm packages, enabling persistent access and credential exfiltration from affected build environments.
label: AV-410

title: Checkmarx Identifies PyPI Supply Chain Attack Delivering Malicious Docker Images
description: Checkmarx researchers identified a PyPI supply chain attack where malicious packages deployed Docker images containing API key extraction code, silently collecting and transmitting authentication credentials from developer environments to attacker infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: GitHub Advisory Database Documents Typosquatting Attacks on Machine Learning Packages
description: Multiple GitHub Security Advisories document typosquatting attacks targeting popular machine learning libraries where fake packages with near-identical names log training data and model outputs to external servers, targeting AI and data science developers.
label: AV-200

title: OSV Database Documents Dependency Confusion via Shadow Package Upload Attacks
description: The Open Source Vulnerability database documents dependency confusion attacks where attackers upload shadow packages to public registries matching private internal dependency names, causing build systems to resolve to attacker-controlled packages through version precedence exploitation.
label: AV-509

title: Unit42 Documents Trojan Source Logic Inversion via Unicode Bidirectional Characters
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented Trojan Source attack cases where Unicode bidirectional characters reverse logical conditions in source code, causing security-critical boolean expressions to evaluate to their opposite values while appearing syntactically correct to reviewers.
label: AV-300

title: axios npm Package GitHub Actions Workflow Vulnerable to Pipeline Secret Exfiltration
description: A vulnerability in the axios npm package GitHub Actions workflow allowed attackers to exfiltrate repository secrets by triggering workflow execution with malicious base64-encoded payloads through unsanitized pull request inputs.
label: AV-410

title: noblox.js-proxy-server Malicious Package Delivers Payload Only During Runtime Not Install
description: The malicious noblox.js-proxy-server npm package behaved normally during installation but executed hidden cryptocurrency wallet-stealing and data exfiltration payloads during runtime, evading install-time security scanning by delaying malicious behavior.
label: AV-400

title: OSV Database Documents Malicious PyPI Packages with Legitimate Functionality as Cover
description: The Open Source Vulnerability database documents malicious PyPI packages that provide expected functionality to appear legitimate while silently logging user data, API responses, and environment variables to external servers throughout the package lifecycle.
label: AV-200

title: Socket.dev Documents Dependency Confusion with Partial Internal Resolution Failures
description: Socket.dev researchers documented dependency confusion attacks where partial internal registry failures cause specific transitive dependencies to resolve from public registries, allowing attackers to target indirect dependencies rather than top-level packages.
label: AV-509

title: BleepingComputer Documents Mixed Trojan Source Attacks Combining Valid and Hidden Logic
description: BleepingComputer documented sophisticated Trojan Source attacks that mix legitimate functional code with hidden Unicode-based manipulation, making the malicious modifications significantly harder to detect than pure obfuscation attacks.
label: AV-300

title: Checkmarx Documents Conditional Poisoned Pipeline Execution Activating Only in Production
description: Checkmarx researchers documented conditional Poisoned Pipeline Execution attacks where malicious CI steps only activate under specific environment conditions such as production release builds, significantly reducing detection probability during development testing.
label: AV-410

title: tj-actions Supply Chain Attack Delivers Malicious Remote Configuration to 23000 Repositories
description: Attackers compromised the tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action to deliver malicious remote configuration payloads that activated data-stealing behavior, affecting over 23,000 repositories that had pinned to vulnerable version tags.
label: AV-400

title: OSV Database Documents npm Malicious Packages Using Visually Similar Character Swaps
description: The Open Source Vulnerability database for npm documents multiple malicious packages using visually similar Unicode character substitutions in package names to impersonate trusted libraries, exploiting developer inattention during package installation.
label: AV-200

title: Phylum Documents Dependency Confusion Targeting Nested Transitive Dependencies
description: Phylum researchers documented dependency confusion attacks specifically targeting indirect transitive dependencies rather than top-level packages, exploiting the fact that nested dependency names are less visible and monitored by security teams.
label: AV-509

title: PHP CodeSniffer Extra Addresses Trojan Source Hidden Inside Code Comments
description: The PHPCSExtra project addressed Trojan Source vulnerabilities where malicious logic could be embedded inside source code comments using Unicode encoding tricks, causing PHP static analysis tools to misparse and potentially execute hidden malicious directives.
label: AV-300

title: Snyk Documents GitHub Actions Supply Chain Attacks Triggering Only on Release Builds
description: Snyk researchers documented GitHub Actions supply chain attacks where malicious workflow steps conditionally activate only during production release builds, evading detection in development and staging pipelines while compromising final release artifacts.
label: AV-410

title: Datadog Documents npm Packages Using Delayed Execution to Evade Security Scanning
description: Datadog Security Labs documented malicious npm packages that delay malicious execution using timers or usage counters, triggering harmful behavior only after repeated use to evade automated install-time and runtime security scanning tools.
label: AV-400

title: Unit42 Documents Malicious PyPI Typosquatting Packages with Full Legitimate Documentation
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented sophisticated malicious PyPI typosquatting packages that include complete legitimate documentation, changelogs, and README files to appear credible, significantly increasing installation rates among targeted developers.
label: AV-200

title: ReversingLabs Documents Dependency Confusion Exploiting Private Registry Fallback
description: ReversingLabs researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting private registry fallback mechanisms where build systems automatically query public registries when private packages are temporarily unavailable, allowing attackers to intercept dependency resolution.
label: AV-509

title: OCaml Community Addresses Trojan Source Unicode Bidirectional Text Perception Attack
description: The OCaml community documented Trojan Source vulnerabilities where Unicode bidirectional text formatting characters alter how code appears during review versus how it executes, enabling hidden logic that bypasses security controls invisible to human code reviewers.
label: AV-300

title: StepSecurity Documents Nx Build System Compromise via Environment Variable Injection
description: StepSecurity documented a supply chain security incident where the popular Nx build system package was compromised with data-stealing malware that injected malicious code through manipulated environment variables in CI/CD pipeline executions.
label: AV-410

title: eslint-scope Compromise Demonstrates Slow Data Exfiltration Alongside Legitimate Functionality
description: The compromised eslint-scope npm package demonstrated a slow data exfiltration pattern where the package continued performing its legitimate linting functions while gradually leaking npm authentication tokens and credentials to attacker infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: JFrog Documents Malicious PyPI Packages Using Unicode Homoglyph Typosquatting
description: JFrog researchers documented malicious PyPI packages using Unicode homoglyph characters in package names that are visually indistinguishable from legitimate library names, deceiving developers and automated dependency management tools into installing malware.
label: AV-200

title: SecurityWeek Documents Dependency Confusion Attacks via Mirrored Repository Mismatches
description: SecurityWeek reported on dependency confusion attacks exploiting mismatches between mirrored repositories and original sources, where public mirrors override trusted internal package sources affecting Apple, Microsoft, and other major technology organizations.
label: AV-509

title: NVD Documents CVE-2021-42574 Trojan Source Modifying Control Flow via Unicode
description: The National Vulnerability Database documents CVE-2021-42574 where invisible Unicode bidirectional control characters in source code modify control flow execution paths, causing programs to execute fundamentally different logic than what appears in the source file during review.
label: AV-300

title: GitHub Security Lab Documents Encoded Variable Payload Execution in GitHub Actions
description: GitHub Security Lab documented pipeline poisoning attacks where malicious payloads encoded in base64 are stored in environment variables and decoded at runtime within GitHub Actions workflows, evading static workflow analysis and secret scanning tools.
label: AV-410
